prompt.content: Write a letter addressed to the parents or guardians of Mason Brown stating his trimester 1 grade; lowest scoring assignments; averages for homework, assessments, and classwork and how the weights of the low scores could be impacting their overall grade; what other school services they're receiving; number of tardies and unexcused absences; reiteration of the importance of consistent attendance as we move to the next unit; and two relevant strategies to implement for the next unit on To Kill a Mockingbird. Round final numerical values to a tenth of a percentage

golden_responses.golden_text_response:
Dear Parents/Guardians of Mason Brown, 

I hope this letter finds you well. I am writing to you today to discuss trimester 1 grades for English Language Arts. Mason is a bright student, but I have noticed a pattern of low grades in this class. My records show that he ended the trimester with a grade of 66.3%. Mason’s lowest scoring assignments were 59.0% for a figurative language quiz and argument writing diagnostic. His averages for assessments, homework, and classwork are 67.0%, 63.6%, and 66.1% respectively. 

I know that Mason is utilizing school resources for his dyslexia and I am committed to ensuring his success in my class. I want to propose two other strategies to add into the mix that can be implemented as we head into our next unit covering To Kill A Mockingbird:

* Guided writing support: This involves using speech-to-text tools to help with writing responses, as well as modeling how to cite textual evidence, and provides sentence frames or graphic organizers.

* Utilizing multiple modalities for reading: An audiobook version will be provided to students so Mason can listen and follow along with the physical book. I will also encourage more micro-chunked reading of 1-2 chapters at a time followed by comprehension questions to solidify understanding. 

I also wanted to reiterate the importance of regular attendance. The unit we are entering will be challenging, and it is important that students are present and on-time to class in order to avoid falling behind. My records show that Mason does have two tardies and one unexcused absence, but going forward I know that we can work together to set a goal to have no tardies or unexcused absences in the next trimester. 

I am committed to Mason’s success as we move through the school year. Should he need any further assistance, don’t hesitate to reach out to me.

Sincerely, 

Mrs. A
